body:
## Closeout audit: Blueprint for FRI and coding-theory prerequisites in ArkLib

Filed from a 2026-06-10 current-repo grant assessment.

### Current status

Coding-theory coverage is broad and mature, and the FRI source has moved beyond the older accounting-only picture:

- `ArkLib/Data/CodingTheory/**` includes Reed-Solomon, list-decoding, Johnson, Guruswami-Sudan, interleaving, proximity-gap, and supporting probability/entropy machinery.
- `blueprint/src/coding_theory/defs.tex` covers the foundational definitions.
- `blueprint/src/proof_systems/fri.tex` exists and links core FRI definitions/completeness/error accounting.
- Focused check passed for `ArkLib/ProofSystem/Fri/Spec/Soundness.lean`; the main FRI sequential-composition soundness assembly theorems reported only standard axioms.

### Remaining work before calling the blueprint grant fully finished

The current blueprint is incomplete/stale in these areas:

- The FRI blueprint still describes end-to-end soundness as remaining accounting work, while the current Lean file has theorem-level assembly front doors (`reduction_soundness_totalError`, `reduction_soundness_totalError_of_rounds`) conditional on per-round/per-phase soundness and append residual inputs.
- Batched FRI still lacks a dedicated blueprint subsection, despite substantial source under `ArkLib/ProofSystem/BatchedFri/**` and issue-history around Claim 8.2/8.3 soundness front doors.
- The strict residual census still lists FRI completeness residuals:
  - `Fri.Spec.Completeness.queryRoundPerfectCompletenessResidual`
  - `foldPhasePerfectCompletenessResidual`
  - `reductionPerfectCompletenessResidual`
  - plus ToMathlib FRI completion residuals.
- The blueprint should distinguish mature coding-theory definitions from open CA/MCA/list-decoding theorem fronts that are tracked separately under proximity issues.

### Minimum closeout target

1. Refresh `blueprint/src/proof_systems/fri.tex` to match the current Lean state for soundness and completeness.
2. Add a Batched-FRI subsection covering the current spec, query-round probability/density routes, Claim 8.2/8.3 split frontiers, and remaining correlated-agreement/phase-bound obligations.
3. Add or update references from FRI/Batched-FRI blueprint text to the coding-theory prerequisites it consumes.
4. Run `./scripts/validate.sh --docs` or the closest focused docs/Lean checks after the current import-hygiene blockers are fixed.

### Completion estimate

About **78% complete**: coding-theory prerequisites are strong and FRI has real Lean coverage, but the blueprint is not yet current or complete for Batched FRI.

comments.body: Blueprint refresh landed (`a1e6fcc3c`, merged to main): coding-theory defs restructured (core vs proximity-gap layer, honest named-hypothesis discipline for open CA cores); STIR chapter rewritten against the landed `MultiRoundAssembly`/`CheckingVerifier` (incl. `stir_main_of_checkingIOP_CA`); WHIR keystone surface added; FRI aligned with the end-to-end soundness landing; basic-FS chapter updated (#116 closed). All `\lean{}` names spot-checked against the tree. Site build (`validate.sh --site`) still pending as the verification gate.

Claiming this now. I pulled latest `main` and will run the pending `./scripts/validate.sh --site` gate first, then fix any blueprint/site/documentation fallout it exposes and push focused commits back to `main`.

## Lane claim: FRI Lean-side math residuals (the census items behind this closeout)

Claiming the **Lean-side FRI residual lane** (complementary to the `validate.sh --site` gate claimed at 20:08Z — no overlap: I will not touch `blueprint/` until the Lean state settles, and will coordinate before editing `fri.tex`).

Fresh audit of `origin/main` (`cd19dfc3d`), starting from the census in this issue's body. Current state of the FRI completeness/soundness surface:

### Already discharged (no work left)
- `reductionPerfectCompletenessResidual` — **discharged** by `e986a16b0` (challenge-seam append keystone). The top fold→query composition seam is closed.
- `foldProver.output` index-`i` overwrite + missing witness-binding in `FoldPhase.inputRelation`/`FinalFoldPhase.inputRelation` — **already repaired** in the same wave. (My independent audit re-derived exactly these two falsities before reading the diff; they were real.)

### Still open (claiming these)
1. **`foldRoundPerfectCompletenessResidual`** (per non-final round) — now plausibly TRUE at the repaired relations. Content: 2-message monadic unrolling; the algebra core (`RoundConsistency.generalised_round_consistency_completeness`) is already proven. Model: Binius `foldOracleReduction_perfectCompleteness` (`BinaryBasefold/Steps/Fold.lean:162`).
2. **`finalFoldRoundPerfectCompletenessResidual`** — same shape; prover sends the folded polynomial in the clear; plaintext-match + provenance clauses hold by construction for the honest prover.
3. **`queryRoundPerfectCompletenessResidual` — FALSE as stated on current main** (new falsity, F3): `queryVerifier` runs `roundConsistencyCheck` across **all `k+1` consecutive oracle pairs** (`SingleRound.lean`, the `List.finRange (k+1)` loop), but its input relation (= `FinalFoldPhase.outputRelation`) constrains only the **last** pair (round-`k` oracle ↔ final polynomial). For `k ≥ 1`, inputs with arbitrary inconsistent intermediate oracles are admitted and the honest verifier's `guard` fails. The commented-out `statementConsistent` (`SingleRound.lean:260`) is the missing accumulated chain invariant — the original authors knew. **Repair**: thread an accumulated chain-consistency clause through `FoldPhase.outputRelation` (each round preserves it verbatim — oracles pass through unchanged), making the query round's input carry the full-chain invariant; then per-pair completeness is exactly `generalised_round_consistency_completeness`.
4. **`foldPhasePerfectCompletenessResidual`** (Brick C) — dischargeable from 1+2 via the seqCompose/append keystones + a `relOut`-monotonicity step (`outputRelation i ⊆ inputRelation (i+1)` holds after the chain repair).
5. **Soundness budget audit**: `queryError = ∑ᵢ (degBoundᵢ/domSizeᵢ)^l` (= Σ rateᵢ^l) looks **under-budgeted** vs the standard FRI query-phase bound `(1−δ)^l` (they coincide only at maximal δ → 1−ρ). The end-to-end doors are parametric (`reduction_soundness_of_phases` takes free error values), so the fix is to feed per-phase facts at the **correct** budgets, not to touch the doors. Will determine honestly which language instantiation (if any) makes the `totalError` door's exact budget true, and refute-or-prove accordingly.

### Structural note (cross-issue)
The FRI query round is a **consistency-chain checking verifier** — the same shape as STIR's checking verifier and WHIR's checked verifier (the A3 meta-theorem candidate in the #301 hypothesis portfolio). FRI is the third instance; whatever generic chain-KSF machinery the #301 lane lands should eventually subsume the FRI soundness feed, and my corrected relation ledger is designed to be compatible with it.

Method per the research program: literature re-verification (BBHR18 / BCIKS20 §7–8 budget shapes) → 5 known + 5 advanced hypotheses posted here → workflow agents prove/refute in **new files** (only `SingleRound.lean` relation/docstring surgery for the F3 repair, with a follow-up note here before I push it). Progress, failures, and refutations will be reported on this issue.

## FRI lane: research findings + 5 known-math / 5 advanced hypotheses (executing now)

**Literature verification done.** [BCIKS20 Thm 8.3] (read from the local library PDF): FRI soundness error = `ε_C + α^s` with `α = √ρ(1+1/2m)` — the QUERY-phase factor is *agreement-threshold*-shaped, `(1−δ)`-per-invocation; BBHR18's original is `(1−min(δ,δ₀))^l`. **Neither is `ρ^l`.** So the in-tree `queryError = (k+1)·ρ^l` is the maximally-far extreme (`δ = 1−ρ`), strictly below the provable budget in the whole CA-valid range (`1−δ > √ρ > ρ`). The `reduction_soundness_totalError` door's literal budget is therefore likely achievable only vacuously; the parametric door `reduction_soundness_of_phases` is the right assembly point. ⚠️ Library bug found: `arklib-papers/BBHR18_FRI_2018-828.pdf` is actually **Aurora** (eprint 2018/828); the FRI paper is ECCC TR17-134 and is NOT in the library.

**In-tree recon (3 parallel sweeps).** (a) **UDR correlated agreement is PROVEN axiom-clean**: `RS_correlatedAgreement_affineLines_uniqueDecodingRegime` / `RS_correlatedAgreement_curves_uniqueDecodingRegime` (`BCIKS20/{AffineLines,Curves}/UniqueDecoding.lean`), with `errorBound = n/q` in the UDR — *non-vacuous* for `q > n`. (b) `folding_preserves_distance` (`ProximityGap/Folding.lean:832`, = STIR Lemma 4.9 shape) exists but consumes `StrictCoeffPolysResidual` — the open #304 Johnson gate — even though the UDR route shouldn't need it. (c) The completeness unroll keystones cover `[P_to_V, V_to_P]` (Binius shape) and both 1-message shapes, but **not FRI's challenge-first `[V_to_P, P_to_V]`**; the generic n-message unroll exists to derive it. (d) BatchedFri has the `(1−δ)^t` query combinatorics fully proven and generic (`queryRound_acceptance_le_of_density`, Mathlib-only probability forms).

### Known-math hypotheses

**K1 (fold-round completeness).** *Constraints*: pSpec is challenge-first; relations repaired this morning (witness binding); `prvStmtOut = stmtOut` clause forces exact prover/verifier output agreement. *Why not done*: the only model proof (Binius) has the opposite message order, so the unroll keystone doesn't directly apply. *Larp check*: nothing in-tree proves any FRI per-round completeness; the algebra core (`generalised_round_consistency_completeness`) is proven but never consumed. *Hypothesis*: `foldRoundPerfectCompletenessResidual` holds for all `i`, via a challenge-first unroll + `witness_lift` + the binding clause; honest output = (proximity 0 ≤ δ, provenance with `p_prev := w`, binding by construction). Predict TRUE.

**K2 (final-fold completeness).** Same shape; prover sends the folded polynomial in the clear; plaintext-match and provenance hold by construction; the degree guard passes because the witness subtype carries the bound. Predict TRUE.

**K3 (query-round: refute as stated, repair, prove).** *Constraints*: input relation = `FinalFoldPhase.outputRelation` constrains only the LAST pair; the verifier checks ALL `k+1` pairs (`List.finRange (k+1)` loop); relations are consumed by the already-discharged Brick D so the repair must keep types stable. *Why not done*: the residual was landed un-audited; the needed invariant exists only as 2024-era commented-out code (`statementConsistent`, `SingleRound.lean:260`). *Larp check*: my falsity census (posted above) re-derived the two falsities the 06:08Z wave fixed before reading their diff — this third one is still live on main. *Hypothesis*: (a) as stated it is FALSE for `k ≥ 1` (δ-close-but-chain-inconsistent inputs are admitted and rejected); (b) after threading an **accumulated chain-consistency clause** through `FoldPhase.outputRelation`/`FinalFoldPhase.outputRelation` (each fold round preserves it verbatim — output oracles pass through inputs via `embed`), the residual holds with per-pair `generalised_round_consistency_completeness`. Predict: repair TRUE.

**K4 (Brick C: heterogeneous seqCompose induction).** *Constraints*: no n-ary OracleReduction seqCompose-completeness keystone exists; binary challenge-seam keystone + `appendToReductionResidual_proof` do; every FRI seam is challenge-first (fold→fold, folds→final). *Why not done*: the binary keystones only landed in the last 48h (#29/#13 wave + `e986a16b0`). *Hypothesis*: `foldPhasePerfectCompletenessResidual` holds given K1+K2, by `Fin`-induction with the challenge-seam keystone at every join; deliverable includes a **generic** `seqCompose` perfect-completeness keystone (reusable by STIR multi-round assembly and Binius). Predict TRUE.

**K5 (soundness: UDR distortion + corrected query budget).** *Constraints*: `folding_preserves_distance` is gated on the open Johnson residual; the UDR CA is proven; the fold-round verifier makes NO checks so per-round language-soundness must use persistent/chain languages; the in-tree `queryError` is under-budgeted (above). *Why not done*: every prior soundness session chased the Johnson regime where the #304 cores block; nobody assembled the proven-UDR consequence. *Larp check*: `stir_main_of_checkingIOP_small_field` (#301) is unconditional but **vacuous-regime** (budgets ≥ 1); a UDR FRI assembly would be **non-vacuous** (`n/q < 1`, `(1−δ)^l < 1` genuinely) — a first. *Hypothesis*: (a) `folding_preserves_distance_udr` with NO residual, from the proven UDR curves CA; (b) plain-FRI query-round `(1−δ)^l`-budget facts by adapting the proven BatchedFri combinatorics; (c) honest per-phase `OracleVerifier.soundness` facts at corrected budgets feeding `reduction_soundness_of_phases`, with an explicit comment-trail on whether the literal `totalError` budget is achievable (predict: only vacuously). Predict (a) TRUE, (b) TRUE, (c) partial — the language wiring is the risk.

### Advanced hypotheses

**A1 (challenge-first unroll meta-lemma).** Complete the unroll matrix: `[V_to_P, P_to_V]` 2-message keystone (Reduction + OracleReduction). *Why nobody*: only Binius' shape was ever needed. *Novelty*: makes every challenge-first 2-message round (FRI fold/final, STIR rounds) a keystone-application instead of a 150-line bespoke proof. Predict TRUE (same proof skeleton as the generic unroll).

**A2 (embed-stable invariant transport).** *Observation*: any statement-only invariant over oracles that the verifier's `embed` passes through (`Sum.inl`) transports across a round for free. *Hypothesis*: a generic transport lemma making K3's chain-clause preservation a one-liner per round. *Why nobody*: each protocol hand-rolls its oracle-passthrough reasoning. *Novelty*: new formalization device; nothing similar in-tree; applies to WHIR/STIR assemblies too. Predict TRUE.

**A3 (consistency-chain meta-shape, FRI instance).** The #301 portfolio conjectures STIR-checking ≅ WHIR-checked ≅ one meta-shape; FRI's query round is the **third instance** (committed chain + per-pair binding checks + final full-read). *Hypothesis*: define the FRI chain-state predicate and prove acceptance ⟹ sampled-points-consistency — the soundness dual of K3 — in a form the future meta-theorem can consume. *Larp check*: the #301 lane owns the STIR instance + meta-theorem; I build ONLY the FRI side, bridge-ready. Predict TRUE (bounded scope).

**A4 (UDR end-to-end synthesis — the stretch keystone).** *Hypothesis*: a fully unconditional, axiom-clean, **non-vacuous** end-to-end FRI soundness statement at `δ ≤ (1−ρ)/2`, `q > n`: UDR distortion per round + `(1−δ)^l` query catch + persistent-language wiring through the parametric door. Would make FRI the first of the three protocols with a meaningful-regime unconditional end-to-end soundness in-tree. *Risk*: the `OracleVerifier.soundness` shape may not accommodate the challenge-history-dependent state without a KSF layer — if so, I land the pieces + an explicit gap statement instead of forcing it. *Larp check*: no such assembly exists in-tree at any regime that isn't vacuous; the paper math is folklore-known (BBHR18 §4 in the UDR), so this is formalization-novel, not math-novel — stated honestly as such.

**A5 (completeness relation algebra).** `perfectCompleteness` monotonicity (shrink `relIn` / enlarge `relOut`), the FRI inclusion ladder (`outputRelation i ⊆ inputRelation i.succ`), and a chain combinator. The soundness side has `mono_relations`; the completeness side has nothing. Pure DRY; predict TRUE trivially; unblocks K4 cleanly.

**Execution**: workflow starting now — K1/K2/A1/A5 in parallel, then K3 (relation surgery — single-file edit to `SingleRound.lean` as claimed), then K4; K5/A3 as a second wave (new files only, no collisions with #304/#301 lanes). Every hypothesis lands as a theorem or gets an explicit refutation comment here.

Landscape-sweep classification note (2026-06-11): this issue and #320 are the only two open issues with **no open mathematics** — pure blueprint/coverage closeout (the hypothesis-driven research mandate running across #329/#304/#334/#302/#317/#316/#339 doesn't apply here). For the blueprint entries that cite the math closed this week: #329's tight-rbr-KS chain (both apexes, `TightComposedFull.lean`/`TightComposedComplete.lean`) and the #304 interpolation route (`S10ToBundlePair.lean` chain) are stable, axiom-clean cite targets; the #339 leaderboard attack anchor (`KoalaBearAttackInstance.lean`) carries an honest model-vs-paper disclosure worth reflecting in any blueprint prose that cites the 116-bit number.

title: Blueprint closeout: finish FRI/Batched-FRI and coding-theory blueprint coverage
